title: Bitfarms Completes Stronghold Digital Mining Acquisition
text: Bitfarms finalized acquisition of Stronghold Digital Mining on March 17, 2025 through stock-for-stock merger. Stronghold shareholders received 2.52 Bitfarms shares per share owned, with approximately 59.7 million Bitfarms common shares and 10.6 million warrants issued. Additional $44.5 million paid to retire Stronghold's outstanding debt. Combined entity now manages 623 megawatts including 165 MW of active generating capacity and 142 MW of immediately available grid import capacity. Acquisition secures 1.1 GW growth pipeline across three Pennsylvania sites within PJM market. CEO Ben Gagnon stated deal makes company industry leader in PJM market and positions company to leverage combined operational expertise and balance sheet strength in Pennsylvania-New Jersey-Maryland region. Year-end 2025 energy portfolio projected to shift to 80% North American and 20% international operations. Strategic partners WWT and ASG prioritizing Stronghold sites for high-performance computing and artificial intelligence development with potential capacity reaching nearly one gigawatt across two campuses. Company gains nearly one exahash through existing Canaan hosting agreements with 50% profit sharing, bringing total capacity to 18 exahash under management.
